purpose: Provide training material and metadata guidance for Zora creator posts, receipts, and future review bundles without authorizing any mint.

core_fields.instruction: Use a short human-readable title that explains the artifact or post candidate.
State the purpose, evidence boundary, and whether the item is draft, review, or executed.
Use Jay Wisdom / @jaywisdom where appropriate and keep identity references consistent.
Record Base when the post, coin, or mint is intended for Base.
Use explicit states such as DRAFT, REVIEW, AUTHORIZED, EXECUTED, or NOT_MINTED.
Reference repo artifacts, commit SHAs, transaction hashes, or Zora URLs only when they exist.

metadata_rules: Do not imply a mint happened unless a mint transaction or platform receipt exists.
Do not imply authority from a draft, bundle index, candidate post, or coin page.
Keep public-facing descriptions clear enough for humans and structured enough for agents.
Prefer stable references: commit SHA, artifact path, profile handle, chain, and timestamp.
Separate creator narrative from verification evidence.

zora_post_template.title: Jay Wisdom Proof Artifact
zora_post_template.description: A documented L4 creator artifact for Base/Zora review. Publication is not execution. Draft is not mint. Human approval required before irreversible action.
zora_post_template.creator: Jay Wisdom / @jaywisdom

training_objectives: Teach agents to distinguish draft metadata from executed mint evidence.
Teach reviewers to preserve creator identity, chain context, and receipt lineage.
Teach future workflows to require explicit human authorization before Zora mint execution.
Teach metadata writers to avoid narrative drift and unsupported authority claims.
next_lawful_moves: Reference this metadata guide from the L4 bundle index.
Use the template when revising ZORA_POST_CANDIDATE_001.
Open a mint authorization receipt only after explicit human approval.
